Peter Fenwick and Elizabeth Fenwick, The Truth in the Light (London: Headline, 1997), 25–26. 2. Corliss Lamont, The Illusion of Immortality (New York: Continuum, 1990), 76. 3. Corliss Lamont, The Illusion of Immortality (New York: Continuum, 1990), 86–108. 4. Lamont’s portrayal of psychic research is extremely superficial, and contains several false and misleading statements. For an excellent critique of Lamont’s book, exposing a mass of inconsistencies and non sequiturs, see C. J. Ducasse, A Critical Examination of the Belief in a Life after Death (Springfield, IL: Thomas, 1961), chap. 13. 5. F. C. S. Schiller, Riddles of the Sphinx (London: Swan Sonnenschein, 1891), 295. 6. Cyril Burt, ESP and Psychology (London: Weidenfeld and Nicolson, 1975), 60. CONTRIBUTORS Allowah has been exploring consciousness for a long time. In his early teens he took to running and philosophy, reading voraciously/neurotically, and disdaining most other human company, culminating in what in retrospect might have been a kundalini awakening at nineteen that left him in bliss for months. That was his initiation into actual experience of the deeper dimensions of existence/consciousness. He is a teacher at the Sunshine State Yoga Academy in Naples, Florida. Researcher and author Chris Carter received his undergraduate and master’s degrees from the University of Oxford. The author of Parapsychology and the Skeptics and Science and the Near Death Experience , Carter is originally from Canada and currently teaches internationally. Erik Davis is a writer, award-winning journalist, and teacher based in San Francisco. He is the author, most recently, of Nomad Codes: Adventures in Modern Esoterica . He also wrote The Visionary State: A Journey through California’s Spiritual Landscape with photographs by Michael Rauner, as well as the books Led Zeppelin IV and Tech-Gnosis: Myth, Magic, and Mysticism in the Age of Information , which has been translated into five languages. His essays on art, music, technoculture, and contemporary spirituality have appeared in well over a dozen books, and Davis has contributed articles and essays to a variety of publications, including Bookforum, ArtForum, Salon, Blender , the LA Weekly , and the Village Voice . He hosts the podcast Expanding Mind on the Progressive Radio Network and is currently pursuing a PhD at Rice University. Paul Devereux is an experienced and respected author and researcher primarily dealing with archaeological themes and ancient lifeways, unusual geophysical phenomena, and consciousness studies. While making his subject matter attractive and accessible to a wide audience, his material is factually based. He has written or co-written twenty-five books since 1979. He has also written a range of peer-reviewed academic papers. Based in the Cotswolds, England, he is a research fellow in the International Consciousness Research Laboratories (ICRL) group in Princeton; a full member of the Society for Scientific Exploration (USA); a member of the Scientific and Medical Network; a member of the Folklore Society; a member of the Traditional Cosmology Society; a member of the Society for Psychical Research; and a member of the Society for the Anthropology of Consciousness (USA). Jennifer Dumpert is a San Francisco–based writer and lecturer and the founder and leader of the experiential dream group Oneironauticum. She has taught classes on dreams as adjunct faculty at Pacifica Graduate Institute. Some of her lectures on dreams can be viewed at www.urbandreamscape.com . She posts daily to Twitter: @OneiroFer. Conner Habib is a writer, gay porn star, and teacher. He’s pretty sure he’s the only person who’s won awards for all three of those things. He studied creative writing as well as organismic and evolutionary biology at the University of Massachusetts. He lives in San Francisco, where he spends most of his time reading, writing, and running a Rudolf Steiner and anthroposophy discussion group. His blog is www.connerhabib.wordpress.com , and you can also follow him on Twitter (@ConnerHabib) if you’re eighteen or older. His adult site (18+ only, contains adult content) is www.connerhabib.com . Paul Hughes is the executive director of Vivation International. He is an internationally recognized speaker, empowerment teacher, firewalking instructor, futurist, systems thinker, writer, visionary artist, juggler and freelance human being. Paul has dedicated his life to the service and uplifting of humanity. You can find all of Paul’s past and present writings at www.enthea.org . Paul lives near Lake Tahoe, Nevada. Ryan Hurd is an independent consciousness researcher located in Philadelphia, PA. His work centers around re-invoking the imaginal realms in a post-rational age. Author of The Lucid Immersion Blueprint and Sleep Paralysis , Ryan is editor of www.DreamStudies.org and faculty at www.TheDreamTribe.com . He is also a board member of the International Association for the Study of Dreams and a member of the Society for the Anthropology of Consciousness. Franklin LaVoie is a visionary artist, with a background in earth science education. Franklin underwent intense shamanic initiations throughout the 1970s to become a natural healer. His research appears in a wide variety of books, including, most recently: The 2012 Story , by John Major Jenkins (2009); Shadows of the Western Door , by Mason Winfield (1997); and Planet Heartworks , by Daniel Winter and Friends (1989). Franklin’s essay “Why Nature and The Arts Can Heal Us” (2007) was included in The Society for the Arts in Healthcare’s international conference in 2009. Franklin designs stage sets, mechanical contraptions, and musical instruments, and he’s written plays, short stories, and poetry. He is preparing to publish The Sacred Geography of Buffalo-Niagara . A pioneer in the field of spiritual emergence, in 1981 Paul Levy was drafted into a higher-dimensional shamanic initiation process which mimicked psychosis but was an experience of a far different order. A healer, Paul is in private practice helping others who are also spiritually awakening. As the ex–bookstore manager of the C. G. Jung Foundation of New York, he has been studying Jung’s work for close to thirty years. An artist, Paul is a long-time Tibetan Buddhist practitioner. He is the author of The Madness of George W.
